title: phormium tenax var. variegatum
intro: Phormium tenax var. variegatum, also known as New Zealand flax, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its striking, variegated leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phormium tenax var. variegatum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as to support digestive health.
